RULING:
DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON PLAINTIFFS' CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant John Crane Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from sustaining its burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of presenting evidence that conclusively negates an element of plaintiffs' punitive damages claim. Even if the Court considers all of defendant's evidence to which plaintiffs object, it is not sufficient to sustain defendant's burden. Defendant failed to demonstrate that public policy dictates that its motion should be granted. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Kim Richardson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5915 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

RULING:
DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE INC.'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE TO RIVERSIDE COUNTY

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant John Crane Inc.'s motion to transfer venue to Riverside County is denied. The motion is denied on the proper court ground because it is untimely. Defendant filed an answer and did not file a motion to transfer at that time. (C.C.P. 396b(a), 397(a).) Even if the Court could consider a discretionary transfer pursuant to C.C.P. 397(a), defendant failed to meet its burden of demonstrating that San Francisco is not the proper court. Finally, even assuming San Francisco is not the proper court, defendant may only move to change venue to its residence and defendant did not show that its residence is in Riverside County. As to C.C.P. 397(c), defendant failed to meet its burden of demonstrating that the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice will be promoted by transfer. Order signed in open court.
